title: Fix expiration bypass: enforce real-time expiration checks on downloads and block expired attachments on backend

requirements.text: On the backend, when a request is made to retrieve or download an attachment or image blob, check whether the parent paste has expired before returning any data. If the paste is expired, refuse to serve the attachment and return an error (e.g., #expired or equivalent variant). Do not rely solely on UI-side expiration gating.

quotes: Data availability after expiration: I'll ensure that when a paste expires, the backend actually deletes (or refuses to serve) the attached files and images, not just blocks the UI.

acceptanceCriteria: A direct backend call to retrieve a blob/attachment for an expired paste returns an expiration error, not the blob data.
The expiration check is performed at the time of the retrieval request, not cached from a prior state.
Both file attachments and image data are subject to the same backend expiration enforcement.

requirements.text: On the frontend, in PasteViewPage.tsx, before triggering a file or image download, perform a fresh expiration check against the backend (re-fetch or re-validate the paste) at the moment the user clicks the download button. If the paste is found to be expired at that point, abort the download and display the expired error state instead of proceeding with the download.

quotes: suppose if i set the paste expiration time is 10 minute. now page is open but it's more than 10 minute. if user click download he is able to download.

acceptanceCriteria: If a paste expires while the page is open and the user clicks download, the download is blocked and the expired error state is shown.
The expiration check at download time queries the backend fresh, not from stale in-memory or cached state.
If the paste is still valid at download time, the download proceeds normally without disruption.

constraints: Do not change the paste creation, password protection, or history features.
Do not alter immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, and components/ui.
nonGoals: Automatic deletion of blob data from storage on expiration (refusing to serve is sufficient).
Changes to expiration duration options or the expiration UI.
Any changes unrelated to expiration enforcement on downloads.
